name: Trail to the Virgin of the Peña
name_original: Ermita de la Virgen de la Peña – running desde Puebla de Guzmán
description: Run 8.3 miles to the Ermita de la Virgen de la Peña from Puebla de Guzmán for panoramic views and varied terrain.
region_intro: Jogging around Puebla De Guzmán offers diverse landscapes with alternating plains and small hills, providing varied terrain for runners. The region features Mediterranean vegetation and significant natural landmarks such as Cerro del Águila, which offers expansive views. Runners can explore unique environments like the "lunar landscape" of Mina de São Domingos with its gravel tracks, or enjoy routes by the Barragem da Tapada Grande reservoir. The area's natural parks also provide pedestrian paths through abundant trees and shrubs, creating serene backdrops for jogging routes. Best jogging routes around Puebla De Guzmán The most popular jogging route is Trail to the Virgin of the Peña, a 8.3 miles (13.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 40 minutes to complete. This moderate route ascends towards the historic Hermitage of Nuestra Señora de la Peña, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local runners is Rest area by the lake loop from Parque Estacionamento da Praia fluvial da Tapada Grande, a moderate 4.4 miles (7.1 km) path. This route provides a pleasant environment for running by the Barragem da Tapada Grande reservoir. Local runners also love the Hermitage of the Virgin of the Rock – Hermitage of the Virgin of the Peña loop from Puebla de Guzmán, a 13.8 miles (22.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 59 minutes. Jogging around Puebla De Guzmán is defined by small hills, Mediterranean vegetation, and reservoir-side paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult, longer running routes. More than 95 runners have used komoot to explore Puebla De Guzmán's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running routes around Puebla de Guzmán?
A: The running routes in Puebla de Guzmán offer a varied landscape, characterized by alternating plains and small hills, adorned with Mediterranean vegetation. You'll find routes with smooth gravel tracks, especially around areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2814217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mina de São Domingos&lt;/a&gt;, which features a unique 'lunar landscape'. Some paths also lead through natural parks with abundant trees and shrubs, providing a serene backdrop for your run.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for different fitness levels in Puebla de Guzmán?
A: Yes, the area offers a range of running routes for various abilities. You'll find moderate paths, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32172623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rest area by the lake loop from Parque Estacionamento da Praia fluvial da Tapada Grande&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.4 miles (7.1 km) long. For those seeking a greater challenge, there are more difficult and longer routes, including those with significant elevation changes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31843969" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of the Rock – Hermitage of the Virgin of the Peña loop from Puebla de Guzmán&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 13.8 miles (22.3 km) with considerable ascent.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while jogging in Puebla de Guzmán?
A: Many running routes offer stunning views and pass by notable landmarks. You can ascend towards the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5333422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Nuestra Señora de la Peña&lt;/a&gt; on &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984502817" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail to the Virgin of the Peña&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views. The region also features the unique 'lunar landscape' of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2814217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mina de São Domingos&lt;/a&gt; with its distinctive gravel tracks and copper-colored lakes. Additionally, you might encounter the restored 19th-century Molino de la Horca within a natural park, or enjoy routes by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6319282" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barragem da Tapada Grande reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer panoramic views of the region?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984502817" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail to the Virgin of the Peña&lt;/a&gt; leads to the historic Hermitage of Nuestra Señora de la Peña, situated on Cerro del Águila, which provides expansive views of the Andévalo region, the Sierra de Aracena, the Huelva coast, and even Portugal. Natural parks in the area also offer pedestrian paths with panoramic views of the Andévalo region, creating a scenic backdrop for your run.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Puebla de Guzmán?
A: Yes, several circular running routes are available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32172623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rest area by the lake loop from Parque Estacionamento da Praia fluvial da Tapada Grande&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 4.4 miles (7.1 km) path. Another is the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31843969" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of the Rock – Hermitage of the Virgin of the Peña loop from Puebla de Guzmán&lt;/a&gt;, covering 13.8 miles (22.3 km).

Q: Is parking available near the running trailheads in Puebla de Guzmán?
A: Yes, parking is available at several points. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32172623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rest area by the lake loop&lt;/a&gt; starts from Parque Estacionamento da Praia fluvial da Tapada Grande, indicating dedicated parking. Additionally, the Fuente de Los Enfermos, a restored fountain often used as a resting point, also offers parking facilities.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Puebla de Guzmán?
A: Runners frequently praise the diverse landscapes and the opportunity to combine physical activity with natural beauty and cultural exploration. The varied terrain, from plains to small hills, and unique environments like the 'lunar landscape' of Mina de São Domingos, are often highlighted. The panoramic views from spots like Cerro del Águila are also a significant draw for the komoot community.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running paths suitable for a leisurely jog?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always given, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32172623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rest area by the lake loop from Parque Estacionamento da Praia fluvial da Tapada Grande&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 4.4 miles (7.1 km) path by the Barragem da Tapada Grande reservoir, offer a pleasant and generally less strenuous environment suitable for families. Natural parks in the area also provide pedestrian paths that are ideal for leisurely jogs.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Puebla de Guzmán?
A: Generally, many outdoor trails in natural areas are dog-friendly, especially if kept on a leash. However, it's always recommended to check local signage at trailheads or specific park regulations for any restrictions, particularly in protected areas or during certain seasons, to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Puebla de Guzmán?
A: Puebla de Guzmán, located in the Huelva province of Spain, experiences a Mediterranean climate. Spring (March to May) and Autumn (September to November) generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for running, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. These seasons also showcase the region's natural beauty at its best.

Q: How many running routes are available for exploration around Puebla de Guzmán?
A: There are at least 5 distinct running routes around Puebla de Guzmán documented on komoot, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels for you to explore. These routes have been enjoyed by over 95 runners in the komoot community.

Q: Are there any historical sites accessible via running routes?
A: Yes, several routes allow you to combine your run with a glimpse into local history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984502817" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail to the Virgin of the Peña&lt;/a&gt; leads directly to the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5333422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Nuestra Señora de la Peña&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the restored 19th-century Molino de la Horca is accessible via pedestrian paths within a natural park, offering a historical point of interest during your run.
tips: The Hermitage is located on the so-called Cerro del Águila, about four kilometers from Puebla de Guzmán. It was probably built in the 16th century, although the most important construction works were undertaken in the 17th century.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this running route?
faqs.answer: The route from Puebla de Guzmán to the Ermita de la Virgen de la Peña is approximately 13.3 km (8.3 miles) round trip. Komoot estimates the average duration for jogging this route to be around 1 hour and 40 minutes, but this can vary based on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this running route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, offering a mix of surfaces. You'll encounter sections of 'senda' (path or trail), paved tracks, unpaved tracks, and some asphalt. Closer to the summit, especially on the Cerro del Águila, the path may involve uneven and potentially rocky surfaces due to the 'conglomeration of enormous rocks' mentioned in its description.

faqs.question: Is this running route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate. It involves a significant elevation gain of 283 meters (930 feet) over its length, with an average gradient of 4.4% on the ascent. While not extremely difficult, beginners should be prepared for sustained climbs and varied terrain.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or features along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the primary feature is the destination itself: the Hermitage of the Virgin of the Peña. Perched on the Cerro del Águila, it offers spectacular panoramic views of the Andévalo countryside, the foothills of the Sierra de Aracena, and on clear days, even the coast of Huelva and Portugal. The hermitage itself is a historic 16th-century sanctuary with significant cultural importance.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the running route to Ermita de la Virgen de la Peña?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the trailhead in Puebla de Guzmán are not provided, it is generally advisable to look for public parking within the town itself, close to where the route begins. From there, you can start your run towards the Ermita.

faqs.question: Is the running route to Ermita de la Virgen de la Peña dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding specific dog restrictions for this route is not available. However, as a general rule for trails in natural areas, if dogs are permitted, they should be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to run this trail would generally be during the cooler months of spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are more pleasant for running, and the natural scenery is often at its most vibrant. Summer can be very hot in this region.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to run this route?
faqs.answer: No, there is no mention of any permits or entrance fees required to access or run this route to the Ermita de la Virgen de la Peña. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: Is this running route suitable for families?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty, significant distance (13.3 km round trip), and varied terrain including some rocky sections, this route might be challenging for younger children or families not accustomed to trail running. It is more suited for active families with experience in similar distances and elevation gains.

faqs.question: What kind of footwear is recommended for this running route?
faqs.answer: Due to the varied terrain, which includes unpaved tracks and potentially rocky sections, trail running shoes with good grip and cushioning are highly recommended. This will provide better traction and support compared to standard road running shoes.

faqs.question: Are there any special events at the Ermita that might affect my run?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Ermita de la Virgen de la Peña is a significant pilgrimage destination and hosts a large annual 'Romería'. If you plan to run during this event, expect large crowds, potential road closures, and a very different atmosphere than a typical quiet run. It's advisable to check local calendars for Romería dates if you prefer a less crowded experience.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of the Virgin of the Peña
